title: Ervilia hirsuta
intro: Ervilia hirsuta, also known as hairy evensia, is a perennial herb belonging to the family Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, with reported benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as potential applications in treating wounds and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Ervilia hirsuta include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ervilia hirsuta is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into salves and creams for topical application.
taxonomy: Ervilia hirsuta is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Ervilia. The specific classification of Ervilia hirsuta is Ervilia hirsuta (Willd.) DC. It is a member of the tribe Loteae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants.
